devanagari: आत्मनः संसारबंधो देहाध्यासादस्तीति बोधनं । जगतोमिथ्यात्व निरूपणं च । अथ दशमोऽध्यायः । मयोदितेष्ववहितः स्वधर्मेषु मदाश्रयः । वर्णाश्रमकु...
english_devnagari: ātmanaḥ saṃsārabaṃdho dehādhyāsādastīti bodhanaṃ jagatomithyātva nirūpaṇaṃ ca atha daśamo mayoditeṣvavahitaḥ svadharmeṣu madāśrayaḥ varṇāśramakulācāramakāmātmā samācaret
translation:
The Lord said:
Alertly observant of the righteous duties (prescribed in the Pāñcarātra and other Vaiṣṇavite systems) taught by me, completely dependent on me, one should, without entertaining any desire (for the fruits of his actions), observe the course of duties prescribed for one’s respective social class (varṇa), stage of life (āśrama) and family traditions[1].
purport:
Footnote [1]:
EK. classifies Karma into four categories: nitya (to be performed daily), naimittika (occasional), Kāmya (performed for fulfilment of desire) and Prāyaścitta (expiatory). All these should be dedicated to the Lord. EB. 10.46-66.

devanagari: अन्वीक्षेत विशुद्धात्मा देहिनां विषयात्मनाम् । गुणेषु तत्त्वध्यानेन सर्वारम्भविपर्ययम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: anvīkṣeta viśuddhātmā dehināṃ viṣayātmanām guṇeṣu tattvadhyānena sarvārambhaviparyayam
translation: With his mind purified (by the observance of his prescribed duties—dharma), he should (always) note how all the endeavours of embodied beings, who, believing objects of sense pleasures to be real[2], are addicted to them and are frustrated, leading them to the contrary results.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
tattva-jñānena (i) Realizing and contemplating the real nature of his soul and the Supreme Soul—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) Definitely ascertaining the means of attaining Puruṣārthas (objects of human life)—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: सुप्तस्य विषयालोको ध्यायतो वा मनोरथः । नानात्मकत्वाद्विफलस्तथा भेदात्मधीर्गुणैः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: suptasya viṣayāloko dhyāyato vā manorathaḥ nānātmakatvādviphalastathā bhedātmadhīrguṇaiḥ
translation: Just as the objects seen in the dream-state, or the hallucinations of a day-dreamer have no existence in reality and are futile (as they present both desirable and undesirable things unrelated to the main objective of human life), similarly, the notion of difference (in relation to Ātman as a celestial being, human being) caused by Guṇas is illusory or meaningless.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Padaratnāvalī takes a contrary view: Just as dream-visions....and fancies of day-dreamers are different from reality and hence fruitless, similarly the notion of one-ness or identity is baseless as it is the apprehension of the faculties of the mind, but not of the essential-powers of the self.

devanagari: निवृत्तं कर्म सेवेत प्रवृत्तं मत्परस्त्यजेत् । जिज्ञासायां सम्प्रवृत्तो नाद्रियेत्कर्मचोदनाम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: nivṛttaṃ karma seveta pravṛttaṃ matparastyajet jijñāsāyāṃ sampravṛtto nādriyetkarmacodanām
translation: A person who is solely devoted to me should perform disinterestedly[4] the obligatory (e.g. Sandhyā, pañcamahāyajñas and occasional (e.g. Śrāddha for ancestors) types of acts but should abstain from acts (to be performed for the fruits thereof)[5]. But a person who has entered into the investigation of the nature of the Soul or god need not respect and follow the injunctions of the Vedas, prompting the performance of (sacrificial and other) acts.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Padaratnāvalī defines Nivṛtta Karma as that done with a correct understanding of Paramātman and done without any desire for return:
niṣkāmaṃ jñānapūrṇaṃ ca nivṛttam iti co'cyate

Footnote [5]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes a Smṛti text as authority. It means: ‘A person desirous of Liberation, from saṃsāra should not turn to kāmya (promising fruits in return) and niṣiddha (prohibited) karmas but only the daily obligatory (nitya) and occasional types of acts (karmas)’.

devanagari: यमानभीक्ष्णं सेवेत नियमान्मत्परः क्वचित् । मदभिज्ञं गुरुं शान्तमुपासीत मदात्मकम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: yamānabhīkṣṇaṃ seveta niyamānmatparaḥ kvacit madabhijñaṃ guruṃ śāntamupāsīta madātmakam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śam class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ śam class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation: One who has exclusively devoted himself to me should continuously practice the five vows (such as non-violence, truth, possessionlessness) known as Yama and occasionally vows (such as penance, charity, silence) called Nīyama. He should wait upon his spiritual preceptor, who is full of serenity, has realized me (as his Self) and is devoted to me.

devanagari: अमान्यमत्सरो दक्षो निर्ममो दृढसौहृदः । असत्वरोऽर्थजिज्ञासुरनसूयुरमोघवाक् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: amānyamatsaro dakṣo nirmamo dṛḍhasauhṛdaḥ asatvaro
translation: Such a disciple should be free from pride, envy; he should be alert, watchful, free from attachment. He should be patient, thoroughly devoted to the preceptor, keen on knowing the truth; he should not try to find fault with others and should not waste of his words unnecessarily.

devanagari: जायापत्यगृहक्षेत्र स्वजनद्रविणादिषु । उदासीनः समं पश्यन्सर्वेष्वर्थमिवात्मनः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: jāyāpatyagṛhakṣetra svajanadraviṇādiṣu udāsīnaḥ samaṃ paśyansarveṣvarthamivātmanaḥ
translation: He should be indifferent and unattached to his wife, children, house, lands, relatives, property, etc.[6] He should realize that all his purpose is common to all.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains that as Ātmā abides in all bodies and thus all share the same purpose, viz. happiness, etc., there is no propriety of claiming them exclusively as his own. This leads to dispassion.

devanagari: विलक्षणः स्थूलसूक्ष्माद्देहादात्मेक्षिता स्वदृक् । यथाग्निर्दारुणो दाह्याद्दाहकोऽन्यः प्रकाशकः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: vilakṣaṇaḥ sthūlasūkṣmāddehādātmekṣitā svadṛk yathāgnirdāruṇo dāhyāddāhako
translation: The Soul is distinct from both the subtle and the gross bodies.[7] He is the seer (the witness) and self-illuminating even as the fire which illumines and burns is different from the fuel that, is burnt and lighted up.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Padaratnāvalī: The individual Soul and the Supreme Soul are different and distinct interse from the body and other products of non-intelligent Prakṛti.

devanagari: निरोधोत्पत्त्यणुबृहन्नानात्वं तत्कृतान्गुणान् । अन्तः प्रविष्ट आधत्त एवं देहगुणान्परः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: nirodhotpattyaṇubṛhannānātvaṃ tatkṛtānguṇān antaḥ praviṣṭa ādhatta evaṃ dehaguṇānparaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ bṛh ], [vocative single from √ bṛh ]
[nominative single from √ bṛh ], [vocative single from √ bṛh ]
[accusative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Just as a fire which has entered into the wood and is burning it, assumes the qualities i.e. states of that piece of wood such as coming into existence, going out of existence, its smallness and bigness and its varieties of forms (but is itself distinct from the fuel), so does the Ātman which is distinct from the body, assume the states of the body[8] (Though he has no birth, death, growth, size, forms, etc.)
purport:
Footnote [8]:
Padaratnāvalī: This is the view of the ignorant who identify the individual Soul and the Supreme Soul.

devanagari: योऽसौ गुणैर्विरचितो देहोऽयं पुरुषस्य हि । संसारस्तन्निबन्धोऽयं पुंसो विद्याच्छिदात्मनः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: yo saṃsārastannibandho
translation: For what is called the body is created out of the (three) guṇas of (the Māyā of) Lord Puruṣottama and the saṃsāra (the cycle of birth and deaths) is the consequence of the false identification of the Soul with the body. It is only through the real knowledge of the (distinctness between the body and the) Ātman that this misapprehension of saṃsāra is removed.[9]
purport:
Footnote [9]:
EK. compares the soul involved in Saṃsāra to a king who dreams that he is a beggar. When in the morning, the family priest comes and wakes him up he realizes that he is a king and not a beggar as he regarded himself to be so long. The spiritual preceptor is the priest and the Jīva involved in Saṃsāra is the dreaming King. When instead of aham (I), he realized So'ham (I am He—Brahman) then the misapprehension of Saṃsāra is dispelled.—EB. 10 303-321.

devanagari: तस्माज्जिज्ञासयात्मानमात्मस्थं केवलं परम् । सङ्गम्य निरसेदेतद्वस्तुबुद्धिं यथाक्रमम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmājjijñāsayātmānamātmasthaṃ kevalaṃ param saṅgamya nirasedetadvastubuddhiṃ yathākramam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active first single], [aorist active first single]

translation: This being the case, by means of deep investigation (into reality), a person should properly realize that the absolute, transcendental soul abiding in the product (viz. his body) is different from the body, and then gradually dispel the. misapprehension that this phenomenal world is real.

devanagari: आचार्योऽरणिराद्यः स्यादन्तेवास्युत्तरारणिः । तत्सन्धानं प्रवचनं विद्यासन्धिः सुखावहः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: ācāryo tatsandhānaṃ pravacanaṃ vidyāsandhiḥ sukhāvahaḥ
translation: The spiritual preceptor is the supporting (lower) piece of wood and the pupil is the supported (upper) piece. Instruction is the process of friction (or joining together of these pieces), and the knowledge is the fire that brings happiness[10]
purport:
Footnote [10]:
An echo of Tait. Upa.:
ācāryaḥ pūrva-rūpam / antevāsī uttara-rūpam /
vidyā sandhiḥ / pravacanaṃ sandhānam /
  —Tait. Upa. 1.3.2.
According to Padaratnāvalī, this knowledge burns down the grass in the form of the notion of identity between Jīva and [Brahm?].
(Jīvasya Brahma—bhāva—buddhi—tṛṇa—dahanam /)

devanagari: वैशारदी सातिविशुद्धबुद्धिर्धुनोति मायां गुणसम्प्रसूताम् । गुणांश्च सन्दह्य यदात्ममेतत्स्वयं च शाम्यत्यसमिद् यथाग्निः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: vaiśāradī sātiviśuddhabuddhirdhunoti māyāṃ guṇasamprasūtām guṇāṃśca sandahya yadātmametatsvayaṃ ca śāmyatyasamid yathāgniḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ śam class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ śam class 4 verb], [locative single from √ śam class 4 verb]

translation:
This highly purified[11] intellect (of the capable disciple) (when sharpened by the spiritually capable teacher) drives off the Māyā which is caused by Guṇas. Burning down (i.e. dispelling) guṇas (objects of senses and senses) and the universe that is created by them, this intellect too, automatically disappears like fire, when the fuel is completely consumed.
14-16[12]. If you believe in the multiplicity of these agents or doers of Karmas and the enjoyers of the (consequent) pleasures and pains, and in the eternity and separateness of the world, the Time, The Śāstras and individual Souls (as the Mīmāṃsakas do), if you hold that all objects have a continuity (or eternity) while they undergo constant change, and that the intelligence becomes changed according to the forms of different objects perceived by it, O Uddhava, even if this be the case, due to frequent (forced) associations (of the Soul) with different bodies and due to the force of the division of Time, all embodied souls are repeatedly subjected to the different states of births, deaths, etc.
purport:
Footnote [11]:
Vaiśāradī—pertaining to Viṣṇu who terminates the Saṃsāra characterised by pleasure and pain—Padaratnāvalī

Footnote [12]:
Padaratnāvalī: (The gist only) The distinction between all jīvas is natural, and according to their mutual difference, they are invested with different bodies and the character of their knowledge differs naturally. Jīvas of the highest order are eternal, and from the point of tḥcir body, they are said to be bora; they enjoy only happiness while the second and third may experience a mixture of happiness and misery or only misery.
Bhāgavata Candrikā: Though the Pravṛtti Karma may be the means of achieving Puruṣārthas, all embodied beings are subjected to the six states of body (such as birth, youth, death and the consequent miseries) according to the species in which they are born, due to the influence of Time and its division.

devanagari: अथैषां कर्मकर्तॄणां भोक्तॄणां सुखदुःखयोः । नानात्वमथ नित्यत्वं लोककालागमात्मनाम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: athaiṣāṃ karmakartṝṇāṃ bhoktṝṇāṃ sukhaduḥkhayoḥ nānātvamatha nityatvaṃ lokakālāgamātmanām

devanagari: मन्यसे सर्वभावानां संस्था ह्यौत्पत्तिकी यथा । तत्तदाकृतिभेदेन जायते भिद्यते च धीः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: manyase sarvabhāvānāṃ saṃsthā hyautpattikī yathā tattadākṛtibhedena jāyate bhidyate ca dhīḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle second single], [present passive second single]

devanagari: एवमप्यङ्ग सर्वेषां देहिनां देहयोगतः । कालावयवतः सन्ति भावा जन्मादयोऽसकृत् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: evamapyaṅga sarveṣāṃ dehināṃ dehayogataḥ kālāvayavataḥ santi bhāvā janmādayo

devanagari: तत्रापि कर्मणां कर्तुरस्वातन्त्र्यं च लक्ष्यते । भोक्तुश्च दुःखसुखयोः को न्वर्थो विवशं भजेत् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāpi karmaṇāṃ karturasvātantryaṃ ca lakṣyate bhoktuśca duḥkhasukhayoḥ ko nvartho vivaśaṃ bhajet
translation: Even here (from your argument) it becomes obvious that there is absence of free will in the case of doers of actions and there is no choice for the experieṇcers of pleasures and pains. What happiness can there be to one who is dependent (and at the mercy of others)?

devanagari: न देहिनां सुखं किञ्चिद्विद्यते विदुषामपि । तथा च दुःखं मूढानां वृथाहङ्करणं परम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: na dehināṃ sukhaṃ kiñcidvidyate viduṣāmapi tathā ca duḥkhaṃ mūḍhānāṃ vṛthāhaṅkaraṇaṃ param
translation: [13] Not an iota of real happiness exists for those embodied souls who are learned (and expert in the technique of performing Vedic Karmas; nor is misery the lot of the ignorant; Hence, the sense of pride (of knowing the correct technique of performance of Karma) is vain and futile.
purport:
Footnote [13]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā: Experts in the technique of performance of Karmas find it very difficult to collect materials, etc. at the time of performance of the Karmas and arc nervous due to the transient nature of its fruits. Or Even great gods like Brahmā do not enjoy unmixed happiness, as they are aware of the end of the tenure of their office. But fools entertain futile egotism about it.
Padaratnāvalī: Even the wise, being attached to their bodies, have no real happiness. What need be said of the ignorant proud persons?

devanagari: यदि प्राप्तिं विघातं च जानन्ति सुखदुःखयोः । तेऽप्यद्धा न विदुर्योगं मृत्युर्न प्रभवेद्यथा ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: yadi prāptiṃ vighātaṃ ca jānanti sukhaduḥkhayoḥ te

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ jñā class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ jñā class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ jñā class 9 verb]

translation: Even though some people happen to know the means of obtaining happiness and averting miseries (and the principle of Free Will be accepted), even such knowers are ignorant of the means of counter-acting (successfully) the power of Death.

devanagari: कोऽन्वर्थः सुखयत्येनं कामो वा मृत्युरन्तिके । आघातं नीयमानस्य वध्यस्येव न तुष्टिदः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: ko āghātaṃ nīyamānasya vadhyasyeva na tuṣṭidaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ yat class 10 verb], [locative single from √ yat class 10 verb]

[nominative dual from √ yat class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ yat class 10 verb], [vocative dual from √ yat class 10 verb], [accusative dual from √ yat class 10 verb], [locative single from √ yat class 10 verb]

[nominative single from √ yat class 10 verb], [nominative dual from √ yat class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ yat class 10 verb], [vocative dual from √ yat class 10 verb], [accusative dual from √ yat class 10 verb]

translation: What achievement of object or a desire can give happiness when Death is near him? No object can give pleasure to a victim who is being led to the place of execution.

devanagari: श्रुतं च दृष्टवद्दुष्टं स्पर्धासूयात्ययव्ययैः । बह्वन्तरायकामत्वात्कृषिवच्चापि निष्फलम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutaṃ ca dṛṣṭavadduṣṭaṃ spardhāsūyātyayavyayaiḥ bahvantarāyakāmatvātkṛṣivaccāpi niṣphalam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śru class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ śru class 5 verb]
[nominative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb]

translation: The heavenly happiness promised by Vedas is also vitiated with envy, jealousy, termination and decay, as in the case of pleasure experienced on the earth. And there are many impediments and chances of failure in the accomplishment of desires as in the case of Agriculture.

devanagari: अन्तरायैरविहितो यदि धर्मः स्वनुष्ठितः । तेनापि निर्जितं स्थानं यथा गच्छति तच्छृणु ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: antarāyairavihito yadi dharmaḥ svanuṣṭhitaḥ tenāpi nirjitaṃ sthānaṃ yathā gacchati tacchṛṇu
translation: Even if a religious act is properly performed without any impediment in its successful conclusion, listen to the nature of the celestial position attained by him.

devanagari: इष्ट्वेह देवता यज्ञैः स्वर्लोकं याति याज्ञिकः । भुञ्जीत देववत्तत्र भोगान्दिव्यान्निजार्जितान् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: iṣṭveha devatā yajñaiḥ svarlokaṃ yāti yājñikaḥ bhuñjīta devavattatra bhogāndivyānnijārjitān
translation: Having worshipped gods (like Indra, Varuṇa, etc.) by performance of sacrifices in this world, the sacrificer goes to the celestial world. He enjoys like a god heavenly pleasures that he has earned through his meritorious acts.

devanagari: स्वपुण्योपचिते शुभ्रे विमान उपगीयते । गन्धर्वैर्विहरन्मध्ये देवीनां हृद्यवेषधृक् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: svapuṇyopacite śubhre vimāna upagīyate gandharvairviharanmadhye devīnāṃ hṛdyaveṣadhṛk
translation: In a lustrous celestial car (provided with all luxuries) earned by his meritorious acts, he, wearing a rich, attractive dress, sports with the celestial damsels while being glorified by Gandharvas in song.

devanagari: स्त्रीभिः कामगयानेन किङ्किणीजालमालिना । क्रीडन्न वेदात्मपातं सुराक्रीडेषु निर्वृतः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: strībhiḥ kāmagayānena kiṅkiṇījālamālinā krīḍanna vedātmapātaṃ surākrīḍeṣu nirvṛtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb]

translation: While sporting with the heavenly nymphs in a celestial car which is adorned with small jingling bells, and which goes according to the will of the occupant, he is so absorbed in celestial pleasures that he is not aware of his impending fall.

devanagari: तावत्स मोदते स्वर्गे यावत्पुण्यं समाप्यते । क्षीणपुण्यः पतत्यर्वागनिच्छन्कालचालितः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tāvatsa modate svarge yāvatpuṇyaṃ samāpyate kṣīṇapuṇyaḥ patatyarvāganicchankālacālitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ puṇ class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ puṇ class 10 verb]
[nominative dual from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ pat class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ pat class 1 verb], [locative single from √ pat class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb]

translation: He heartily enjoys himself in the heaven so long as the fund of his merits is not exhausted. But when the balance of merits is exhausted, he is pushed down by Time and he falls down reluctantly.

devanagari: यद्यधर्मरतः सङ्गादसतां वाजितेन्द्रियः । कामात्मा कृपणो लुब्धः स्त्रैणो भूतविहिंसकः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: yadyadharmarataḥ saṅgādasatāṃ vājitendriyaḥ kāmātmā kṛpaṇo lubdhaḥ straiṇo bhūtavihiṃsakaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ lubh class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ lubh class 6 verb]

translation: If, due to the association with unrighteous people, a person takes delight in impiety or loses control over his senses, being passionate and libidinous, he becomes miserly, avaricious and addicted to women and heartlessly injurious to living beings.

devanagari: पशूनविधिनालभ्य प्रेतभूतगणान्यजन् । नरकानवशो जन्तुर्गत्वा यात्युल्बणं तमः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: paśūnavidhinālabhya pretabhūtagaṇānyajan narakānavaśo janturgatvā yātyulbaṇaṃ tamaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yaj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ yaj class 1 verb]

translation: He kills animals in violation of Śāstric injunctions and worships the hosts of goblins and ghosts therewith. Such a person inevitably falls into various hells and ultimately enters the hell of terrible darkness (i.e. is born as an immobile).

devanagari: कर्माणि दुःखोदर्काणि कुर्वन्देहेन तैः पुनः । देहमाभजते तत्र किं सुखं मर्त्यधर्मिणः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: karmāṇi duḥkhodarkāṇi kurvandehena taiḥ punaḥ dehamābhajate tatra kiṃ sukhaṃ martyadharmiṇaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: What happiness can there be to a mortal person who, in the present birth (as a human being) perpetrates deeds that result in miseries and in consequence of which acts, he has to assume another body?

devanagari: लोकानां लोकपालानां मद्भयं कल्पजीविनाम् । ब्रह्मणोऽपि भयं मत्तो द्विपरार्धपरायुषः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: lokānāṃ lokapālānāṃ madbhayaṃ kalpajīvinām brahmaṇo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ mad class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ mad class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ mad class 4 verb]

translation: Regions and their rulers whose span of life extends to the end of a Kalpa (432 million years) are afraid of me. Even god Brahmā whose life is of two Parārdha years fears Me.

devanagari: गुणाः सृजन्ति कर्माणि गुणोऽनुसृजते गुणान् । जीवस्तु गुणसंयुक्तो भुङ्क्ते कर्मफलान्यसौ ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: guṇāḥ sṛjanti karmāṇi guṇo jīvastu guṇasaṃyukto bhuṅkte karmaphalānyasau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ sṛj class 6 verb]

translation: Guṇas i.e. senses and organs create (i.e. are the cause of) Karmas; a guṇa (like Sattva etc.) motivates the senses to actions. This Jīva, being endowed with sense-organs, enjoys the fruits of his acts.

devanagari: यावत्स्याद्गुणवैषम्यं तावन्नानात्वमात्मनः । नानात्वमात्मनो यावत्पारतन्त्र्यं तदैव हि ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: yāvatsyādguṇavaiṣamyaṃ tāvannānātvamātmanaḥ nānātvamātmano yāvatpāratantryaṃ tadaiva hi
translation: So long as the unbalanced condition of guṇas prevails, manifoldness (and difference) will be projected on Ātman (which, as a matter of fact, is one). As long as the (apparent) multiplicity in the Soul remains, the state of dependence (of the Jīva) shall persist.

devanagari: यावदस्यास्वतन्त्रत्वं तावदीश्वरतो भयम् । य एतत्समुपासीरंस्ते मुह्यन्ति शुचार्पिताः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: yāvadasyāsvatantratvaṃ tāvadīśvarato bhayam ya etatsamupāsīraṃste muhyanti śucārpitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ muh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ muh class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ muh class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ ṛ ], [vocative plural from √ ṛ ]
[nominative plural from √ ṛ ], [vocative plural from √ ṛ ], [accusative plural from √ ṛ ]

translation: So long as the dependent state of the Jīva continues, there will be fear from God. Hence, those who take resort to guṇas become overcome with grief and delusion.

devanagari: काल आत्मागमो लोकः स्वभावो धर्म एव च । इति मां बहुधा प्राहुर्गुणव्यतिकरे सति ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: kāla ātmāgamo lokaḥ svabhāvo dharma eva ca iti māṃ bahudhā prāhurguṇavyatikare sati
translation: When there is an imbalance in the three guṇas of Māyā (and there is agitation of the guṇas), ignorant people call me by various names such as Time (Kāla), Soul (Ātman), scriptures (Āgama), the World (Loka), the Nature (Svabhāva) and Dharma.

devanagari: श्रीउद्धव उवाच । गुणेषु वर्तमानोऽपि देहजेष्वनपावृतः । गुणैर्न बध्यते देही बध्यते वा कथं विभो ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīuddhava uvāca guṇeṣu vartamāno guṇairna badhyate dehī badhyate vā kathaṃ vibho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Uddhava asked:
How is it that the Ātman, though associated with and present in the guṇas of the body, is not bound by them? Or if he be independent, how does he get involved in them? (What is the correct position in the matter) O Lord.

devanagari: कथं वर्तेत विहरेत्कैर्वा ज्ञायेत लक्षणैः । किं भुञ्जीतोत विसृजेच्छयीतासीत याति वा ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: kathaṃ varteta viharetkairvā jñāyeta lakṣaṇaiḥ kiṃ bhuñjītota visṛjecchayītāsīta yāti vā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: By what characteristics is a bound or liberated Soul be recognised? How does he behave? With whom is he found sporting or wandering? What things does he enjoy or cast away? How does he lie or sit or go about?

devanagari: एतदच्युत मे ब्रूहि प्रश्नं प्रश्नविदां वर । नित्यबद्धो नित्यमुक्त एक एवेति मे भ्रमः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: etadacyuta me brūhi praśnaṃ praśnavidāṃ vara nityabaddho nityamukta eka eveti me bhramaḥ
translation:
O Acyuta! You are the foremost among those who solve problems. I am really confused. How can the same Ātman be both eternally bound and ever-free? Be pleased to explain this problem to me.
[back to top]
